meta.setting: Brushy oak slope above a deer trail near French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in predator sign, tracking, and livestock pressure

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Mountain lion habitat, ambush behavior, prey signs, livestock conflict, and covered carcass evidence.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer as common prey and sign context.

image_prompt.subject: a mountain lion trail through brush: drag marks, plucked fur, nervous horse ears in the foreground, and a tawny shape high on a rock ledge
image_prompt.scene: late golden light in chaparral and oak shade; deer bones partly covered with leaves, long shadows, dust hanging still, and riders just outside the frame reading the sign
image_prompt.composition: square frame with foreground sign, middle trail, and hidden predator as the final read; the lion is visible only after the evidence leads the eye there
image_prompt.camera: horseback point of view with shallow but game-readable depth, no modern UI or tracking overlay
material_cues: dry leaves over a covered kill
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the lion a punctuation mark, not the sentence. The image should feel watched before it feels dangerous.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine tracking scene
render_traits: slightly simplified cougar, horse, and brush silhouettes
modest texture resolution on fur, dirt, leaves, leather, and rock
hard ambient occlusion under brush, ledge, horse tack, and covered carcass
warm low-angle daylight with deep oak shade
believable 1899 trail gear and natural predator sign
evidence-forward composition rather than attack spectacle
style_modifiers.palette: dry leaf brown, tawny fur, oak shadow, dusty tan, horse leather brown, late sun gold, and rock gray.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, tracking overlays, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The drag marks, fur, horse ears, covered kill, and distant lion are fully readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 trail life and natural predator country.
The cougar is naturalistic and not framed as a monster.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
